    malicious_url ¶

    Classes:

    • MaliciousURL –

      Malicious URLs dataset.

    MaliciousURL ¶

    MaliciousURL()

    Bases: RemoteDataset

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.datasets.malicious_url.MaliciousURL[MaliciousURL]
              vendor.river.river.datasets.base.RemoteDataset[RemoteDataset]

                              vendor.river.river.datasets.base.RemoteDataset --> vendor.river.river.datasets.malicious_url.MaliciousURL
                                vendor.river.river.datasets.base.FileDataset --> vendor.river.river.datasets.base.RemoteDataset

              click vendor.river.river.datasets.malicious_url.MaliciousURL href "" "vendor.river.river.datasets.malicious_url.MaliciousURL"
              click vendor.river.river.datasets.base.RemoteDataset href "" "vendor.river.river.datasets.base.RemoteDataset"
              click vendor.river.river.datasets.base.FileDataset href "" "vendor.river.river.datasets.base.FileDataset"
              click vendor.river.river.datasets.base.Dataset href "" "vendor.river.river.datasets.base.Dataset"

    Malicious URLs dataset.

    This dataset contains features about URLs that are classified as malicious or not.

    References¶

    1. Detecting Malicious URLs ↩

    2. Identifying Suspicious URLs: An Application of Large-Scale Online Learning ↩

    Methods:

    • take –

      Iterate over the k samples.

    Attributes:

    • desc –

      Return the description from the docstring.

    • is_downloaded –

      Indicate whether or the data has been correctly downloaded.

    desc property ¶

    desc

    Return the description from the docstring.

    is_downloaded property ¶

    is_downloaded

    Indicate whether or the data has been correctly downloaded.

    take ¶

    take(k: int)

    Iterate over the k samples.
